title: Prosoc Social Navigation Scenario
description: Schema for a human-authored social navigation scenario card, used to describe contextual situations in which robot navigation behavior is evaluated against the Prosocial Navigation Charter.

id.description: Unique identifier for the scenario. Intended to be stable and suitable for referencing across experiments, datasets, and evaluations.

summary.description: Brief natural-language description of the scenario and its salient features.

scientific_purpose.description: Scientific or evaluative purpose of the scenario (e.g., pedestrian interaction, legibility, safety).

geometric_layout.description: Canonical geometric layout category (e.g., intersection, corridor, sidewalk).

context.description: Environmental, social, and situational context in which the scenario occurs.

environment.description: Physical environment description (e.g., indoor/outdoor, hallway, plaza).

social_setting.description: Social context such as formality, norms, or crowd characteristics.

agents.description: Agents participating in the scenario, including the robot and other entities.

robot.description: Description of the robot agent and its assumed capabilities.

humans.description: Human agents present in the scenario.

attributes.description: Optional human attributes relevant to the scenario (e.g., mobility, attentiveness).

initial_conditions.description: Initial spatial, temporal, or situational conditions at the start of the scenario.

intended_robot_task.description: Assumed task or objective of the robot in this scenario (e.g., navigate from A to B).

intended_human_behavior.description: Assumed behavior of the human(s) in the scenario (e.g., cross navigate, gesture wait).

expected_behaviors.description: Scenario-specific normative expectations describing appropriate and inappropriate robot behavior.

must.description: Behaviors that are required for acceptable performance in this scenario.

should.description: Behaviors that are preferred or encouraged but not strictly required.

should_not.description: Behaviors that are discouraged or indicative of poor performance.

relevant_principles.description: Identifiers of charter principles (e.g., P1, P3) that are particularly relevant to this scenario.

ideal_outcome.description: Concise description of the ideal outcome of the scenario.

related_scenarios.description: Directory names (scenario keys, e.g. 'frontal_approach') of other scenarios in this corpus that are conceptually related (e.g., variants, complements, or contrasts). This is the same key used for the scenario's directory under prosoc/scenarios/, its audit.md frontmatter, and AUDIT_SUMMARY.md rows -- not the versioned scenario.yml 'id' field.

cited_in.description: External works, papers, or reference indices that cite or reference this scenario (e.g., P&G paper citation numbers).

evaluation_notes.description: Additional guidance for evaluators interpreting behavior within this scenario.

scenario_usage_guide.description: Guidance for using this scenario in experiments, evaluation, and dataset labeling, following the Social Navigation Scenario Card structure in the P&G paper.

success_metrics.description: Task-level success metrics used to evaluate whether the scenario was successfully completed (e.g., SR, collisions).

quality_metrics.description: Charter principles used specifically as quality metrics for evaluating behavior in this scenario.

failure_modes.description: Outcome-level failure cases observed when the robot performs poorly in this scenario.

labeling_criteria.description: Criteria used to determine whether an observed episode or trace qualifies as an instance of this scenario, independent of behavior quality.
